title: Introduction to Flight
description: Standard atmosphere, basic aerodynamic theory, airfoil and wing descriptions, introduction to orbital mechanics, elementary aerospace vehicle performance, and experiential introduction to aerospace engineering
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ENGR 102 and PHYS 206 ; grade of C or better in ENGR 216/PHYS 216 or PHYS 216/ENGR 216 ; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 or concurrent enrollment; Aerospace Engineering majors only.

title: Aerospace Engineering Mechanics
description: Fundamentals of Newtonian mechanics; static equilibrium of particles, system of particles and rigid bodies; free body diagrams; rectilinear and curvilinear motion of particles; linear momentum; angular momentum; friction; plane motion of rigid bodies; beams and trusses
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ENGR 102 and PHYS 206 ; grade of C or better in ENGR 216/PHYS 216 or PHYS 216/ENGR 216 ; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 , or concurrent enrollment; Aerospace Engineering majors only.

title: Introduction to Aerothermodynamics
description: Study of thermodynamic properties and processes, heat and work, first and second laws of thermodynamics, ideal cycles for power and refrigeration applications; emphasis on the Brayton jet-engine cycle
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in CHEM 107 and CHEM 117 , or CHEM 120 ; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 , or concurrent enrollment; AERO majors.

title: Introduction to Aerospace Mechanics of Materials
description: Fundamental concepts for deformable bodies (conservation of linear and angular momentum, kinematics and thermoelasticity); notions of stress and strain and illustrative examples for engineering applications; introduction to experimental methods and reporting, instrumentation and uncertainty analysis; measurement of elastic and thermal material properties
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 211 ; grade of C or better in MATH 308 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Analytical Methods for Aerospace Engineering
description: Matrix operations, Gaussian elimination, linear transformations, coordinate transformations, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, diagonalization, including programming and computer implementation, applied to solid mechanics, fluid mechanics, dynamics and control of air and space vehicles
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ENGR 102 , PHYS 206 , and ENGR 216/PHYS 216 or PHYS 216/ENGR 216 ; grade of C or better in MATH 251 or MATH 253 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Introduction to Aerospace Computation
description: Review of basic skills required for developing computer programs and introduction to more advanced concepts in scientific computing to solve aerospace engineering problems; numerical and analytical methods of solving engineering problems involving interpolation and extrapolation; function approximation; numerical differentiation; integration; solutions to linear and non-linear equations and systems of equations; eigenvalues and eigenvectors, numerical integration of differential equations with aerospace engineering applications
prerequisites: Admitted to major degree sequence in aerospace engineering; grade of C or better in AERO 221 ; grade of C or better in MATH 308 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Directed Studies
description: Directed study of special problems in aerospace engineering
prerequisites: Freshman or sophomore classification, or approval of instructor. May be repeated for credit.

title: Special Topics in...
description: Selected topics in an identified area of aerospace engineering. May be repeated for credit
prerequisites: Approval of instructor.

title: Research
description: Research conducted under the direction of faculty member in aerospace engineering. May be taken three times
prerequisites: Freshman or sophomore classification and approval of instructor.

title: Mid-Curriculum Professional Development
description: Participation in an approved high-impact learning practice; reflection on professional outcomes from engineering body of knowledge; documentation and self-assessment of learning experience at mid-curriculum point

title: Theoretical Aerodynamics
description: Conservation of mass and momentum; Reynolds transport theorem; fundamentals of incompressible flow, rotationality, potential flow, superposition, circulation, lift, thin airfoil theory, elliptic wing theory and induced drag
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 201 , AERO 212 ; grade of C or better in AERO 222 and MATH 308 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: High Speed Aerodynamics
description: Fundamentals of compressible flow; governing Euler equations; adiabatic and isentropic flow; normal and oblique shocks and expansion waves, moving shocks, shock interactions, wedge and cone flow; supersonic airfoils, exact and thin theory, choked flow and wind-tunnel design, two-dimensional gas dynamics, and method of characteristics
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 301 .

title: Aerospace Structural Analysis I
description: Three-dimensional beam theory; applications of elasticity theory to beam extension and compression, bending, torsion and transverse shearing; combined loading; analysis of thin-walled structures; stress concentrations; analysis of stability of aerospace structures; elements of structural failure, buckling; structural design considerations
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 214 and MATH 308 ; grade of C or better in AERO 222 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Aerospace Structural Analysis II
description: Work and energy principles; analysis of indeterminate structures by classical virtual work and finite elements; introduction to elastic stability of columns; application of energy methods to determine stresses, strains and displacements in typical aerospace structures; design considerations in aerospace structures
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 304 .

title: Aerospace Engineering Laboratory
description: Intermediate and advanced topics in instrumentation, signal conditioning, data acquisition analysis for aerospace-related measurements; emphasis on technical reporting and data presentation; measurements of materials strain, deformation, pressure, velocity and aerodynamic forces; experimental investigations of static and dynamic response of structures; use of nonintrusive optical techniques; uncertainty analysis; linear regression, Fourier transform and power spectra; tests for statistical significance; design of experiments
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in ENGL 103 or ENGL 104 ; grade of C or better in AERO 222 , AERO 301 , AERO 304 , AERO 310 , ECEN 215 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Aerospace Dynamics
description: Spatial kinematics; general motion of particles; Euler angles; Newton-Euler methods for translation and rotation of rigid bodies; work-energy and impulse momentum principles applied to aerospace systems; Linear theory of free dynamic response of single and multi-degree of freedom systems; frequency response of first and second order systems with instrumentation applications
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 211 and MATH 308 ; grade of C or better in AERO 222 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Dynamics of Aerospace Vehicles
description: Derivation of the nonlinear air and space vehicle dynamics equations; attitude representation with Euler angles and quaternions; forces and moments due to aerodynamics, thrust and gravity gradient; linearization; development of state-space models for aircraft and spacecraft; static and dynamic stability analysis for aircraft and satellites; spin and gravity gradient stabilization; stability derivatives; longitudinal and lateral modes and transfer functions for aircraft; aircraft flying qualities; elements of aircraft configuration design; response to control inputs
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 301 and AERO 310 .

title: Aerothermodynamics and Propulsion
description: Aerothermodynamics of gases; laws of thermodynamics; equilibrium conditions; mixtures of gases; combustion and thermochemistry; compressible internal flows with friction, heat transfer and shock; turbojet cycle analysis and performance; chemical rockets
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 .

title: Aerospace Design Principles
description: Study of systems engineering; project lifecycle; stakeholder, concept of operations (CONOPS) & requirements definition; cost assessment; risk management; trade studies; decomposition and design of an aerospace system; engineering ethics; technical communications
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 306 , AERO 307 , AERO 321 , and AERO 351 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Aerospace Systems Design
description: Continuation of AERO 401 ; detailed design; subsystem integration; realization of the system through prototyping, modeling, and/or simulation; verification and validation through experimentation and analysis; documentation and presentation of results
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 306 , AERO 307 , AERO 321 , AERO 351 , and AERO 401 .

title: Mechanics of Advanced Aerospace Structures
description: Advanced analysis techniques for aerospace structures; material anisotropy, plasticity, fatigue and fracture; laminated materials; solution of plane elasticity, plate and multi-component structural configurations; buckling of beams and plates; application of finite element analysis
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 304 and junior or senior classification.

title: Aerospace Structural Design
description: Overall structural integrity of complete aerospace systems; structures subjected to critical loads; design considerations in aerospace structures
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 306 .

title: Applications of Fracture Mechanics to Aerospace Structures
description: Foundations of linear elastic fracture mechanics of aerospace structure; calculation of stress intensity factors and energy release rates; crack growth under fatigue loading; ASTM standards for fracture testing; the role of fracture mechanics in the analysis and design of aerospace structures
prerequisites: AERO 304 or equivalent with a grade of C or better.

title: Aerospace Materials Science
description: Relationship between aerospace engineering material properties and microstructure; mechanical and thermal properties; environmental degradation; mechanical failure
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 304 .

title: Human Performance in Aerospace Environments
description: Current physiological and psychological aspects affecting human performance during space missions using a quantitative approach and engineering methods
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 .

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics for Aerospace Applications
description: Present methods for solving internal and external flow problems for inviscid and viscous compressible flow; Euler, Navier-Stokes and Large Eddy Simulation solvers, boundary conditions formulation, and basics of parallel processing
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 and AERO 351 .

title: Aircraft Design
description: There are three major phases of aircraft design: (1) conceptual design, (2) preliminary design and (3) detail design. The aircraft design process starts with identifying requirements and performing initial calculations to generate the overall concept of the aircraft. In preliminary design, we design the aircraft’s systems and subsystems. Finally, in detail design, we specify how the aircraft will be fabricated and tested. The course introduces students to tools and methods used in the conceptual and preliminary design of fixed-wing aircraft. Students will learn about aircraft design requirements, aircraft mission, weight estimation, aerodynamics and propulsion analysis, performance analysis, stability characteristics and cost estimation
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 ; grade C or better in AERO 401 , or concurrent enrollment.

title: Aerospace Propulsion
description: Air breathing propulsion; design and analysis of inlets, compressors, combustors, turbines and nozzles; application to aeronautical, aerospace, ground transportation and energy
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 351 .

title: Chemical Rocket Propulsion
description: Nozzles and heat transfer in rockets, liquid/solid/hybrid propellant systems; combustion and combustion stability; flight performance including trajectories, multistaging and flight dynamics; rocket testing
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 351 .

title: Aeroelasticity
description: Classical analysis of fundamental aeroelastic phenomena with application to aerospace vehicles; flutter, divergence, control effectiveness
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 , AERO 306 , and AERO 310 .

title: Active Controls for Aerospace Vehicles
description: Introduction to the Theory of Automatic Control specifically applied to aerospace vehicles; techniques for analysis and synthesis of linear control systems, stability criteria, systems response and performance criteria; design studies of active controls to improve aerospace vehicle performance
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 .

title: Orbital Mechanics
description: Two-body problem, restricted three-body problem, orbital perturbations, orbital maneuvers, interplanetary trajectories, orbit determination and other selected topics
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 310 .

title: Spacecraft Attitude Dynamics and Control
description: Introduces fundamental concepts of satellite attitude dynamics and control; includes derivations of environmental disturbances due to gravity gradient, aerodynamic, and solar radiation pressure; includes treatments of attitude control subsystems, such as thrusters, reaction wheels, CMGs, and magnetic torquers, and their designs
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 .

title: Flight Test Engineering
description: Application of performance and stability and control theory to flight test measurements; standard atmosphere and airspeed equations for pilot-static system calibrations; flight test methods for evaluating performance, stability and control, and stall-spin characteristics; laboratory practice in planning and conducting small flight test project
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 .

title: Space System Design
description: Introduces prevailing practices and processes used in modern space system design; applies knowledge in component engineering disciplines to a design challenge of interest to NASA or DoD; utilizes instruction in systematic methods of design and on dynamics of teamwork; when possible concludes with detailed design using an engineering design facility
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 306 , AERO 321 , AERO 351 .

title: Electromagnetic Sensing for Space-Borne Imaging
description: Study IR and Visible range imaging systems to obtain high resolution imaging of objects from space; this area has numerous applications and areas of advanced development; following instruction in needed background on optics, telescopes, and interferometry, perform preliminary design of imaging system with a different imaging design offered each year
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 306 , AERO 321 , AERO 351 .

title: Numerical Simulation
description: Numerical and analytical simulation of physical problems in sciences and engineering using applied methods; developing and using numerical techniques for physical problems described by nonlinear algebraic equations, ordinary and partial differential equations
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 222 or MATH 417 .

title: Aerothermochemistry
description: Composition of chemically reacting gases (air and propellant); thermodynamic functions based on classical and quantum mechanical theories; calculation of gas temperatures; equilibrium, frozen and non-equilibrium flows through nozzles and shock waves
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 .

title: Cockpit Systems and Displays
description: Design, development, and implementation of cockpit systems and multi-function displays; cockpit system requirements and specifications; human-machine interfaces, Flight Management Systems, navigation and guidance systems; 3-D real-time displays of weather, traffic, and terrain; characteristics and missions of air vehicles; project design and cost analysis
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 or junior or senior classification in computer science.

title: Vehicle Management Systems
description: Introduction to vehicle management systems for manned and unmanned air and space vehicles; system centric concepts, requirements definition, specifications, and architectures; reliability analysis, health monitoring, and mission management; SISO digital design of integrated flight control, propulsion control and structural control; introduction to vehicle autonomy; design and analysis methods, industrial examples
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 422 .

title: Machine Learning for Aerospace Engineers
description: This course discusses fundamental concepts and algorithms in machine learning with a focus on aerospace applications. Students will learn to model, analyze and interpret data arising from flight dynamics, aerodynamics and space systems. The course emphasizes both theoretical understanding and practical implementation using aerospace datasets
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 321 .

title: Human Spaceflight Operations
description: Essential aspects of human spaceflight operations as performed by NASA; in-depth understanding of the state-of-the-art in spacecraft operations, including spacecraft systems, ground and launch operations, mission management and on-orbit activities such as science, robotics, spacewalking and human health maintenance; applications to future space systems
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 310 or equivalent; senior classification.

title: Heat Transfer and Viscous Flows
description: Navier-Stokes and boundary layer equations; exact and approximate solutions; laminar boundary layers; basic concepts of transition and turbulence; turbulent boundary layers; one and two dimensional heat transfer; methods for steady and transient heat conduction; thermal boundary layers; convection; radiation
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 and AERO 351 .

title: Helicopter Aerodynamics
description: Hovering theory, hovering and vertical flight performance, factors affecting hovering and vertical flight performance, auto-rotation in vertical descent, concepts of blade motion and control, aerodynamics of forward flight, forward flight performance, operational envelope and introduction to conceptual design of helicopters
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 222 , AERO 301 , and AERO 310 .

title: Airfoil and Wing Design
description: Subsonic airfoil design and analysis, subsonic wing design and analysis, swept and delta wings, vortex lift, transonic flow methods, viscous transonic phenomena, transonic airfoil and wing design, optimization and advanced topics such as supersonic panel methods
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 .

title: Space Mission Design
description: Analysis of robotic and human space mission design processes, including Low-Earth orbiting missions, planetary orbital missions and robotic and human planetary landing missions; review of spacecraft subsystems, orbital mechanics, and introduction to concept- and detail-level space mission design tools and techniques
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 351 ; grade C or better in AERO 423 or concurrent enrollment.

title: Low Temperature Plasma - Theory, Modeling, Applications
description: Basic theory and fundamentals of low-temperature plasma, elements of plasma kinetic theory, equilibrium and non-equilibrium plasma, modeling approaches, plasma discharges
prerequisites: Grade of C or better in AERO 303 and MATH 308 .

title: Seminar
description: Readings, reports, conferences and discussion. Must be taken on a satisfactory/unsatisfactory basis
prerequisites: Senior classification in aerospace engineering.

title: Directed Studies
description: Special problems in aerospace engineering assigned to individual students or groups
prerequisites: Junior or senior classification or approval of instructor.

title: Special Topics in...
description: Selected topics in an identified field of aerospace engineering. May be repeated for credit
prerequisites: Approval of instructor.

title: Research
description: Research conducted under the direction of faculty member in aerospace engineering. May be repeated 3 times for credit
prerequisites: Junior or senior classification and approval of instructor.
repeatability: May be repeated 3 times for credit
